records.ety: [OE. withoute, withouten, AS. wið<?/tan; wið with, against, toward + <?/tan outside, fr. <?/t out. See With, prep., Out.]

defs: On or at the outside of; out of; not within; as, without doors.
quotes: Without the gate Some drive the cars, and some the coursers rein.

defs: Out of the limits of; out of reach of; beyond.
quotes: Eternity, before the world and after, is without our reach.

defs: Not with; otherwise than with; in absence of, separation from, or destitution of; not with use or employment of; independently of; exclusively of; with omission; as, without labor; without damage.
quotes: I wolde it do withouten negligence.
Wise men will do it without a law.
Without the separation of the two monarchies, the most advantageous terms . . . must end in our destruction.
There is no living with thee nor without thee.
You will never live to my age without you keep yourselves in breath with exercise, and in heart with joyfulness.
notes: ☞ Now rarely used by good writers or speakers.

defs: On or art the outside; not on the inside; not within; outwardly; externally.
quotes: Without were fightings, within were fears.

defs: Outside of the house; out of doors.
quotes: The people came unto the house without.
